I see this in a lot of our code - does it really get executed?  If the activator is not a daemon thread, then this will only be executed if "exit(nnn)" is invoked somewhere.  If the activator IS a daemon thread, then where's the non-daemon thread that keeps it from being invoked right off the bat?

same comment about double logging of message

I'm a little unclear about what's happening here.  The name of this method, and the method on line 144, would suggest that something is being retrieved.  However, line 144 doesn't seem to do anything with any returned value.  Is line 144 getting a value that's discarded?  If so, then seems like a better name on line 140 would be something like "validateStatistics".  OTOH, if line 144 is actually pushing statistics to the listener, then perhaps the name on line 140 should be something like "pushStatistics" and line 144 should be changed to something like processStatistics or handleStatistics.

Either way, the comment on line 135 should be updated to reflect the real intent of the method.

same comment about double logging

same comment applies to subsequent files, too

I'm seeing common code here.  Perhaps we should create one parameterized superclass from which they all subclass.  Then the methods could be removed altogether, and the constructor turned into a single line.  For that matter, if objects of this class are not created using reflection, then the class could be eliminated altogether and the superclass used instead.

this comment applies to other similar classes, including the previous one
why was elementsOnThisParticipant removed?  we want to avoid scanning lists when doing look-ups
maybe combine 294 & 295 into a single forEach()
maybe this should be renamed to canHandle?
what should this really throw?  or maybe it should return a createCommissioningErrorResponse()?

should create a nested BeanValidationResult here, named:

However, I'd also be OK naming it:

  "entry " + controlLoop.getDefinition().getId()


the results added below would then be added to the nested result instead of to validationResult

Variable name should be clElementStatisticsList instead, as its adding statistics to the list and not elements themselves . I am not sure about specifying the size of this particular array list as it would not be elements.size(). Also for my own interest, is there any benefit in providing a particular size for an ArrayList?

I think it is possible code wise for elements to be null. The case should be handled.
This file is not used in JUnits, This is the input configuration file for manually running the runtime component in demo.
Ok, DCAE participant will see more changes in Istanbul, as the current Clamp usage towards DCAE will be changed.

Done. This participant code will be discarded, as this uses CLAMP as a bypass towards DCAE. As this code will not be re-used. I prefer to leave the exception as it is for demo version. Hope that is fine.

Done. This is a demo tweak, to sleep for 10 seconds and recheck if DCAE is deployed. This code is for demo only, and will not be reused for DCAE participant. Here while testing, we are aware that DCAE will be deployed through clamp in 30 seconds, and hence iteration of 100 seconds for demo.
Ok. Have not changed anything here. Can take up in another review. Thank you.

Done. Yes, DCAE participant was not pushed for review earlier. The comments in Policy participant were not handled in DCAE participant. Hence the old code. Corrected now.

This is needed when testcases are run in parallel because of flags in maven surfire plugin. To maintain Internal Participant and ControlLoopElement States, while multiple events are handled at sametime by the handlers. More robust mechanism is needed in Handlers to take care of synchronization while several messages try to manipulate state data. This robustness is not available in demo version.
Done. Reverted to H2 DB. Yes, this config file is used in JUnits.
Done. Could be a copy paste error. Removed this in all logback files.

True, the name of the function is misleading. This function will enable all clElementListeners to retrieve statistics from respective ControlLoopElement, and update the controlloopelement data on Handler accordingly.

another alternative would be to follow the more typical, hierarchical structure of validation results:

Modify the "checkMissing()" method to create a BeanValidationResult, invoking addResult() for each invalid field.  It could then return null, if the result is clean, or the result otherwise.

The validate() method when then simply do two calls like:
  result.addResult(checkMissing("clampClientParameters", clampClientParameters))

use a Map instead

public class SupervisionScanner implements Runnable, Closeable {

If not done, then i will not be able to implement run() method and I also see an error,
"The method scheduleAtFixedRate(Runnable, long, long, TimeUnit) in the type ScheduledExecutorService is not applicable for the arguments (SupervisionScanner, int, long, TimeUnit)"

Hope this is fine.
